how_verified: Verified in isolation (NOT by importing the whole server): /tmp/verify_rule_lane.py + /tmp/verify_rule_attach.py constructed a throwaway FastMCP + a real Suite (FsStore+NodeRegistry over /home/tim/company/nodes, .venv python), called rule.register(mcp, suite), confirmed register() returns the `rule` callable and EXACTLY ['rule'] registers on the throwaway server (no model/GPU needed — rule ops are pure grammar/AST work). Fired a sample of each op: validate (pure read → ok/references/destination_ok/when_text/depth; forbidden destination='dispatch' → ok=False, destination_ok=False), dry_run (fires when recall.relevant>0.5, does-not-fire below), attach + detach against a REAL non-protected role (confirm_registration) → returns a role_build proposal (s1-role_build/s2-role_build) that SURFACES for the operator, never self-applies (floor held). Fail-loud teaching errors confirmed: unknown op (lists valid ops), unknown role (points to cognition_info/list_by_type — registry-is-truth), missing args, and the F5/H2 floor (dry_run on an unresolved declared ref fails loud, never routes-on-missing). Wrapper signatures cross-checked against the live Suite methods at runtime/suite.py:9261 (validate_rule, dry_run_rule@9291, attach_rule@9307, detach_rule@9324) AND against the original flat-tool bodies in git (commit 016cf08 / its parent) — value-conversions reproduced exactly (dict(sample_resolved), dict(params) or None, destination or None / destination or 'inject').
notes: ADOPTION lane, not net-new build. The consolidated rule(op=validate|dry_run|attach) was ALREADY built in a prior beat (git commit 016cf08, 'MCP-SWEEP 4 of 5 lanes') and the 3 flat tools (attach_rule/dry_run_rule/validate_rule) were ALREADY removed from mcp_face/server.py in that same commit — confirmed absent now (grep finds no flat defs; server.py:654 is the consolidation-marker comment). So for the serial removal pass there is NOTHING left to remove for this lane; the flat_tools_to_remove names are reported for completeness/idempotency only. I made NO edits this beat — the existing module already matches the lane spec AND exceeds it with a `detach` op (the symmetric remove of attach, previously direct-only on the Suite; mirrors how corpus.py exposed the previously-unexposed query). File-disjoint preserved: I did not touch server.py, roles/AGENTS.md, or any sibling module. FLOOR held by construction: validate/dry_run are PURE reads (never route()); attach/detach are a CONSTRAINED edit_role that SURFACES a role_build proposal (propose-not-apply, never resolve/approve/dispatch/claude-p), and the destination grammar (runtime/rules.py FORBIDDEN_DESTINATION_VERBS=('resolve','approve','dispatch')) rejects forbidden verbs at build_rule construction. Verification scripts left at /tmp/verify_rule_lane.py and /tmp/verify_rule_attach.py (throwaway, outside the repo).
